metadata.description: Comprehensive robotics skill track from foundations through advanced systems, with prerequisite ordering

nodes.label: What Is a Robot?
nodes.description: Definition of a robot: sense-think-act paradigm. Distinguishing robots from automated machines, key components, and degrees of autonomy.

nodes.label: Types of Robots
nodes.description: Mobile robots, manipulators, humanoids, drones, underwater ROVs, industrial arms, collaborative robots (cobots), and soft robots.

nodes.label: History of Robotics
nodes.description: From ancient automata to modern robotics. Key milestones: Unimate, Stanford Arm, Mars rovers, Boston Dynamics, and the evolution of AI in robotics.

nodes.label: Robot Safety
nodes.description: Safety protocols for working with robots. E-stop mechanisms, safe operating zones, battery safety, mechanical hazards, and ISO safety standards.

nodes.label: Robot Design Process
nodes.description: Engineering design cycle for robots: define requirements, brainstorm, prototype, test, iterate. Systems engineering and trade-off analysis.

nodes.label: Coordinate Systems & Frames
nodes.description: Cartesian, polar, and spherical coordinates. Reference frames, right-hand rule, rotation matrices basics, and spatial reasoning for robotics.

nodes.label: Gears & Gear Ratios
nodes.description: Spur, bevel, worm, and planetary gears. Gear ratio calculations, torque multiplication, speed reduction, and gear train design.

nodes.label: DC Motors
nodes.description: Brushed DC motor operation, torque-speed curves, stall current, back-EMF, gear motors, and motor selection for robot applications.

nodes.label: Servo Motors
nodes.description: Hobby servos, PWM control signal, angular positioning, continuous rotation servos, servo horns, and torque specifications.

nodes.label: Stepper Motors
nodes.description: Stepper motor types (unipolar, bipolar), step angle, microstepping, driver ICs (A4988, DRV8825), and precision positioning.

nodes.label: Actuators & Solenoids
nodes.description: Linear actuators, pneumatic cylinders, solenoids, shape memory alloys, and selecting actuators for specific robotic tasks.

nodes.label: Linkages & Mechanisms
nodes.description: Four-bar linkages, crank-slider mechanisms, cam followers, pantographs, and converting rotary to linear motion for robot appendages.

nodes.label: Bearings, Shafts & Couplings
nodes.description: Ball bearings, bushings, shaft collars, flexible couplings, and managing rotational loads in robot chassis and joints.

nodes.label: Wheels, Tracks & Locomotion
nodes.description: Differential drive, skid steer, omniwheels, mecanum wheels, tank tracks, and comparing locomotion strategies for terrain.

nodes.label: Chassis Design
nodes.description: Robot frame materials (aluminum, acrylic, carbon fiber), structural design, weight distribution, center of gravity, and modularity.

nodes.label: 3D Printing for Robotics
nodes.description: FDM 3D printing, PLA vs PETG vs ABS, designing for 3D printing, tolerances, support structures, and printing custom robot parts.

nodes.label: CAD for Robot Design
nodes.description: Computer-Aided Design basics. Using Fusion 360 or OnShape for robot parts, assemblies, constraints, and exporting STL files.

nodes.label: IR Sensors
nodes.description: Infrared proximity and distance sensors. IR emitter-detector pairs, line-following sensors, analog vs digital output, and reflectance arrays.

nodes.label: Ultrasonic Sensors
nodes.description: HC-SR04 and similar ultrasonic rangefinders. Time-of-flight distance measurement, trigger/echo timing, and obstacle detection.

nodes.label: IMU (Accelerometer & Gyroscope)
nodes.description: Inertial Measurement Units (MPU6050, BNO055). Accelerometer, gyroscope, and magnetometer data. Sensor fusion and complementary filters.

nodes.label: Encoders
nodes.description: Rotary encoders (optical, magnetic). Quadrature decoding, counts per revolution, odometry calculation, and wheel speed measurement.

nodes.label: Camera Modules
nodes.description: USB cameras, Raspberry Pi Camera, OV7670. Resolution, frame rate, field of view, and interfacing cameras with robot controllers.

nodes.label: LiDAR Sensors
nodes.description: Light Detection and Ranging. 2D LiDAR (RPLiDAR), time-of-flight, point clouds, and range scanning for mapping and obstacle detection.

nodes.label: Motor Drivers
nodes.description: H-bridge motor driver ICs and modules (L298N, TB6612, BTS7960). Direction control, PWM speed control, and current sensing.

nodes.label: Power Management
nodes.description: Battery selection (LiPo, NiMH), voltage regulators, power distribution boards, fuses, and managing power budgets for robots.

nodes.label: Wiring & Connectors
nodes.description: Wire gauges, crimping, JST/Dupont/XT60 connectors, wire management, strain relief, and building reliable robot wiring harnesses.

nodes.label: Force & Touch Sensors
nodes.description: Force-sensitive resistors, limit switches, bumper sensors, load cells, and tactile feedback for robotic grippers and collision detection.

nodes.label: GPS Modules
nodes.description: GPS/GNSS receivers for outdoor robotics. NMEA sentences, latitude/longitude parsing, waypoint navigation, and GPS accuracy limitations.

nodes.label: Arduino Programming
nodes.description: C/C++ for Arduino. Variables, loops, conditionals, functions, libraries, serial debugging, and structuring robot control code.

nodes.label: Python for Robotics
nodes.description: Python fundamentals for robotics. Data types, control flow, functions, classes, pip packages, and Raspberry Pi GPIO with Python.

nodes.label: State Machines
nodes.description: Finite state machines for robot behavior. States, transitions, event-driven programming, and implementing FSMs for autonomous tasks.

nodes.label: PID Control
nodes.description: Proportional-Integral-Derivative controller. Tuning Kp, Ki, Kd gains, error correction, setpoint tracking, and PID for line following and balancing.

nodes.label: Sensor Fusion Basics
nodes.description: Combining multiple sensor readings. Complementary filters, weighted averaging, Kalman filter introduction, and improving measurement reliability.

nodes.label: Serial Protocols in Robotics
nodes.description: UART, I2C, and SPI communication between robot subsystems. Multi-board architectures and sensor bus management.

nodes.label: Wireless Remote Control
nodes.description: RC transmitter/receiver, Bluetooth joystick control, WiFi teleoperation, and building custom remote control systems for robots.

nodes.label: Raspberry Pi as Robot Brain
nodes.description: Using Raspberry Pi for robotics. Linux basics, GPIO control, camera interface, running Python scripts, and Pi-Arduino communication.

nodes.label: Line Following
nodes.description: IR reflectance sensor arrays for line following. PID-based steering, intersection detection, and optimizing speed on curved tracks.

nodes.label: Obstacle Avoidance
nodes.description: Reactive obstacle avoidance using ultrasonic and IR sensors. Bug algorithms, potential fields, and real-time decision making.

nodes.label: Odometry & Dead Reckoning
nodes.description: Estimating robot position from wheel encoders. Differential drive kinematics, odometry equations, drift, and error accumulation.

nodes.label: SLAM Basics
nodes.description: Simultaneous Localization and Mapping. Core concept, occupancy grids, scan matching, and introductory SLAM algorithms (GMapping).

nodes.label: Path Planning
nodes.description: A* algorithm, Dijkstra's algorithm, RRT (Rapidly-exploring Random Trees), and planning collision-free paths on known maps.

nodes.label: Computer Vision Basics
nodes.description: OpenCV fundamentals. Image capture, color spaces, thresholding, contour detection, object tracking, and color-based object following.

nodes.label: Fiducial Markers (AprilTags)
nodes.description: AprilTag and ArUco marker detection. Pose estimation from markers, camera calibration, and using fiducials for localization.

nodes.label: Wall Following
nodes.description: Maintaining constant distance from walls using range sensors. PID wall following, corner handling, and maze-solving strategies.

nodes.label: Localization
nodes.description: Determining robot position in a known map. Monte Carlo localization (particle filters), beacon-based localization, and sensor-map matching.

nodes.label: ROS Introduction
nodes.description: Robot Operating System overview. Installation, workspace setup, catkin/colcon build system, ROS 1 vs ROS 2, and core philosophy.

nodes.label: Topics & Services
nodes.description: ROS publish-subscribe (topics) and request-response (services) communication. Message types, custom messages, and QoS settings.

nodes.label: TF Transforms
nodes.description: ROS tf2 library for coordinate frame management. Broadcasting and listening to transforms, static vs dynamic transforms, and URDF models.

nodes.label: URDF Robot Models
nodes.description: Unified Robot Description Format. Defining links, joints, visual/collision geometry, and creating robot models for simulation and visualization.

nodes.label: Navigation Stack
nodes.description: ROS navigation stack (move_base / Nav2). Costmaps, global and local planners, recovery behaviors, and autonomous goal-based navigation.

nodes.label: Gazebo Simulation
nodes.description: Gazebo robot simulator. World files, spawning robots, sensor plugins, physics simulation, and testing algorithms before deployment.

nodes.label: RViz Visualization
nodes.description: ROS 3D visualization tool. Displaying sensor data, robot models, maps, paths, and debugging robot state in real time.

nodes.label: Launch Files & Parameters
nodes.description: ROS launch system for starting multiple nodes. Launch file syntax, parameter server, YAML config files, and composable node containers.

nodes.label: ROS Actions
nodes.description: Long-running goal-oriented tasks. Action servers/clients, feedback, preemption, and using actions for navigation and manipulation goals.

nodes.label: Inverse Kinematics
nodes.description: Computing joint angles to reach a desired end-effector position. DH parameters, forward kinematics, analytical and numerical IK solutions.

nodes.label: Robotic Arms & Manipulators
nodes.description: Multi-DOF robot arms, joint types, workspace analysis, gripper design, pick-and-place operations, and arm control with MoveIt.

nodes.label: Swarm Robotics
nodes.description: Multi-robot coordination without central control. Emergent behavior, flocking algorithms, task allocation, and communication between swarm agents.

nodes.label: Drone Systems
nodes.description: Quadcopter mechanics, flight controllers (Pixhawk, Betaflight), PID tuning for flight, autonomous waypoint missions, and MAVLink protocol.

nodes.label: Machine Learning for Robotics
nodes.description: Neural networks for perception (object detection, classification), reinforcement learning for control, sim-to-real transfer, and training pipelines.

nodes.label: Legged Locomotion
nodes.description: Bipedal and quadrupedal walking. Gait patterns, dynamic balance, zero moment point (ZMP), and compliant leg mechanisms.

nodes.label: Advanced Motion Planning
nodes.description: Trajectory optimization, time-optimal paths, dynamic obstacle avoidance, and motion planning in high-dimensional configuration spaces.

nodes.label: Depth Cameras & 3D Vision
nodes.description: Stereo cameras, structured light (Intel RealSense), time-of-flight depth sensors, point cloud processing, and 3D object recognition.

nodes.label: Human-Robot Interaction
nodes.description: Speech recognition, gesture control, collaborative manipulation, safety around humans, social robotics, and user interface design.

nodes.label: FIRST LEGO League
nodes.description: FLL competition format. LEGO Mindstorms/Spike, mission strategy, attachment design, programming missions, and innovation project.

nodes.label: VEX Robotics
nodes.description: VEX competition system. V5 electronics, VEXcode programming, game analysis, robot design constraints, and alliance strategy.

nodes.label: FIRST Robotics Competition
nodes.description: FRC team structure, game kickoff analysis, robot design in 6 weeks, roboRIO programming (Java/C++), scouting, and match strategy.

nodes.label: RoboCup
nodes.description: RoboCup leagues: soccer, rescue, @home. Autonomous robot teams, multi-agent coordination, computer vision on field, and competition rules.

nodes.label: Combat Robotics
nodes.description: Battle bot design: weapon types (spinners, flippers, crushers), armor materials, drive systems, weight classes, and safety regulations.

nodes.label: Competition Strategy
nodes.description: Game analysis, scoring optimization, alliance selection, scouting systems, match replays, driver practice, and iterative improvement.

nodes.label: Sumo Robotics
nodes.description: Sumo robot competition. Edge detection, opponent detection, pushing strategies, weight and size constraints, and speed vs traction trade-offs.

nodes.label: Maze Solving Robots
nodes.description: Autonomous maze navigation. Wall-following algorithms, flood-fill algorithm, dead-end detection, and speed optimization for micromouse competitions.

nodes.label: Robot Ethics & Society
nodes.description: Ethical considerations in robotics: autonomous weapons, job displacement, privacy, algorithmic bias, and responsible innovation.

nodes.label: Belts & Chain Drives
nodes.description: Timing belts, V-belts, roller chains, and sprockets. Power transmission over distance, tension adjustment, and comparing belts vs gears.

nodes.label: Current Sensing
nodes.description: Shunt resistors, Hall-effect current sensors (ACS712), and measuring motor current for stall detection and power monitoring.

nodes.label: Logic Level Shifting
nodes.description: Interfacing 3.3V and 5V devices. Voltage dividers, MOSFET level shifters, bidirectional level converters, and I2C pull-up considerations.

nodes.label: Debugging Robot Systems
nodes.description: Serial print debugging, LED indicators, logic analyzers, systematic fault isolation, and common failure modes in robotic systems.

nodes.label: Data Logging & Telemetry
nodes.description: Logging sensor data to SD cards, real-time telemetry over wireless, data visualization, and post-run analysis for tuning.

nodes.label: Behavior Trees
nodes.description: Hierarchical task execution model. Selector, sequence, and decorator nodes. Advantages over FSMs for complex robot behaviors.

nodes.label: Occupancy Grid Mapping
nodes.description: Building 2D grid maps from range sensors. Bayesian occupancy updates, map resolution, and storing/loading maps for navigation.

nodes.label: ROS 2 Key Concepts
nodes.description: DDS middleware, lifecycle nodes, component composition, executors, QoS profiles, and migrating from ROS 1 to ROS 2.

nodes.label: Soft Robotics
nodes.description: Compliant and deformable robot bodies. Pneumatic soft actuators, silicone casting, bio-inspired designs, and soft grippers.

nodes.label: Underwater Robotics (ROV/AUV)
nodes.description: Remotely Operated Vehicles and Autonomous Underwater Vehicles. Waterproofing, buoyancy, thruster control, and underwater communication.

nodes.label: Simulation to Real Transfer
nodes.description: Training in simulation and deploying on real robots. Domain randomization, reality gap, digital twins, and validation strategies.

nodes.label: Engineering Notebooks & Documentation
nodes.description: Maintaining engineering notebooks for competitions. Design rationale, test logs, CAD screenshots, code documentation, and judge presentations.

nodes.label: Driver Practice & Skills
nodes.description: Teleop driving proficiency. Controller mapping, practice routines, field awareness, reaction time drills, and driver-operator communication.
